reference: MARMALADES AND JAMS DIFFER LITTLE FROM EACH OTHER THEY ARE PRESERVES OF A HALF LIQUID CONSISTENCY MADE BY BOILING THE PULP OF FRUITS AND SOMETIMES PART OF THE RINDS WITH SUGAR
text: marmalades and jams differ little from each other they are preserves of half-liquid consistency made by boiling the pulp of fruits and sometimes part of the rinds with sugar
